obus 2026.07
  • Reference
    • Catch products: from raw exchange data to survey indices
    • Parquet Files and DuckDB
    • Standardized HL: design rationale and implementation

Standardize HL into a clean catch foundation (length and haul summaries)

dr_HL_standardised.Rd

Produces a unified catch table from raw HH and HL exchange tables with two row types controlled by the type column:

Usage

dr_HL_standardised(hh, hl, species = NULL, haulval = NULL)

Arguments

hh

DATRAS HH table (standard column names, .id present). Required columns: .id, Survey, Year, Quarter, DataType, HaulDuration. Optional: HaulValidity (used when haulval is set).

hl

DATRAS HL table (standard column names, .id present). Required for type = "length": .id, aphia, NumberAtLength, LengthClass, LengthCode, SubsamplingFactor, sex, SpeciesValidity. Required for type = "haul": additionally TotalNumber, SpeciesCategoryWeight, SpeciesCategory.

species

Species lookup with columns aphia, latin, species. Defaults to dr_con("species").

haulval

Character vector of HaulValidity codes to retain. NULL keeps all hauls.

Value

A lazy DuckDB table with columns: .id, Survey, Year, Quarter, aphia, latin, species, type, length_mm, length_cm, accuracy (NA for type = "haul"), n_haul, n_hour, w_haul, w_hour (NA for type = "length"), p_females, SpeciesValidity.

Details

type = "length"

One row per .id \(\times\) aphia \(\times\) length_mm. Sex and DevelopmentStage are collapsed; sex composition is summarised as p_females (proportion female among sexed fish). Derived from NumberAtLength via dr_add_n_and_cpue. Replaces dr_catch_by_length().

type = "haul"

One row per .id \(\times\) aphia. Numbers (n_haul, n_hour) come from TotalNumber; weights (w_haul, w_hour) from SpeciesCategoryWeight. Sex composition summarised as p_females. Replaces dr_catch_total() (without zero-filling). Pass output to dr_catch_by_haul for zero-filling.

Note: n_haul from type = "haul" (TotalNumber) and the haul/species sum of n_haul across type = "length" rows (subsampling-corrected NumberAtLength) represent the same count and should match within rounding. A persistent mismatch indicates an inconsistency in the original DATRAS submission, not a structural feature of the data; see dr_check_totalno.

See also

dr_catch_by_haul, dr_expand_length

On this page

Developed by Einar Hjorleifsson, Martin Pastoors, Vaishav Soni.
